name: provider-runtime-migration
notes: Checklist drafted for large provider-runtime migration.
Checklist confirmed by user with summary: pass.
Todolist created from confirmed checklist.
Implementation slice completed: Claude Code shallow provider, Hermes native API client foundation, existing Codex bridge preserved, project/meeting regressions tested.
Extended implementation: Claude Code chat i18n, server platform/session coverage, Hermes native API detection, Node source-level UI checks.
Hermes native API chat route added as opt-in with CLI fallback; native success, approval, and fallback tests pass.
Runtime HTTP smoke passed on isolated temporary server; browser/CDP checks could not run because 127.0.0.1:9224 was unavailable.
Codex bridge audited: existing codex app-server bridge retained as authoritative, metadata updated to app-server-bridge, tests pass.

Provider runtime config persistence completed: setup/settings round-trip for Hermes native API, Codex, and Claude Code; secret-preserving merge; safe /vo-config exposure; HTTP smoke and regressions pass.

Model runtime visibility and history isolation completed: /config/providers nativeProviders, models.html Native Agents tab, Hermes conversation-scoped history clear, additional regressions and HTTP smoke pass.

Chrome MCP browser smoke completed after user authorized MCP fallback when CDP was unavailable; settings, models Native Agents, and project/meeting surfaces passed fixture-backed smoke with only unrelated /pc-metrics 502 noise.
Final targeted regressions passed after archive update: provider runtime config, Hermes native API/history isolation, and provider runtime settings/model UI source check.
Phase 1 TODO-001 through TODO-025 marked complete for the local-authoritative provider runtime migration scope.

Phase 2 native agent management scope added after user requested continuing the second migration part: selective merge of reference Codex/Claude Code native agent discovery/create/delete/config/UI bottom layer while preserving local project/meeting behavior.
Phase 2 checklist CHK-061 through CHK-085 drafted and waiting for user confirmation before implementation.
Phase 2 todolist TODO-026 through TODO-043 drafted from the new checklist.
Phase 2 checklist confirmed by user with summary: 可以的，继续开发吧.
Phase 2 implementation completed: Codex/Claude Code native agent discovery/create/delete/config support merged selectively under local project/meeting behavior.
Phase 2 tests passed: provider/server/config project/meeting regressions, UI source checks, and isolated HTTP smoke for setup/save plus Codex/Claude Code create/delete lifecycle.

A first smoke attempt wrote temporary Codex TOML files to /home/wo/.codex/agents because codex.homePath was omitted from the payload; those smoke files were removed with approval and the isolated payload was corrected.
Phase 3 requested by user: complete remaining reference provider-runtime parity while preserving local project/meeting behavior.
Phase 3 checklist CHK-086 through CHK-099 and todolist TODO-044 through TODO-054 added and treated as confirmed by the user's direct implementation request.

Phase 3 implementation completed: actionable models Native Agents setup/status UI, setup wizard Codex/Claude native fields, Hermes preferApi compatibility, safe /config/providers expansion, docs/env examples.
Phase 3 tests passed: provider/server/config project/meeting regressions, UI source checks, and isolated HTTP smoke for models/setup native provider save/test flows.

Phase 4 planning requested by user: plan safe merge of reference meeting visualization, agent workspace/project context, and scheduled/cron visibility without bypassing local project/meeting state machines.
Phase 4 review note and checklist CHK-100 through CHK-121 drafted; waiting for user confirmation before creating Phase 4 todolist.
Phase 4 checklist confirmed by user with summary: pass.
Phase 4 todolist TODO-055 through TODO-071 created from the confirmed checklist.
Phase 4 implementation completed: safe targeted merge of reference-derived agent workspace project context while retaining local project/meeting writers and existing reference-derived UI code.

Phase 4 tests passed: new agent workspace read-only API/source tests, project execution, meeting request/blocker, meeting phase1/4/5/6, scheduled cron phase4/5, provider runtime source checks, and Chrome MCP smoke.

Phase 4 residual: tests/test_project_scheduled_cron_phase1.py currently fails only during TemporaryDirectory cleanup because a background cron project directory remains after dispatch; this was documented as non-blocking for the projection change.

Phase 5 requested by user: extract current Codex bridge run/SSE distribution into a generic ProviderRunBridge and let Claude Code/Codex share it while keeping provider-specific protocol adapters and preserving local project/meeting/archive/scheduled behavior.
Phase 5 review note, checklist CHK-122 through CHK-140, and todolist TODO-072 through TODO-086 added; waiting for user confirmation before implementation.
Phase 5 checklist confirmed by user with summary: pass.

Phase 5 implementation completed: ProviderRunBridge owns generic run/SSE distribution, Claude Code delegates to it, Codex exposes additive run/SSE/stop endpoints while preserving existing chat/activity/interaction/cancel paths.

Phase 5 tests passed: py_compile, Codex run bridge source check, Codex run/SSE Python test, Claude Code run/SSE source and Python tests, Codex/Claude provider and server tests, provider runtime config, project execution, meeting blocker, and source-level UI checks.

Phase 6 requested by user: extract approval/interaction, project execution result shape, modifiedFiles/threadId/turnId/active operation semantics into a common office execution contract for Codex and Claude Code, and migrate Codex execution bottom layer closer to the reference branch.
Phase 6 review note, checklist CHK-141 through CHK-160, and todolist TODO-087 through TODO-101 added; waiting for user confirmation before implementation.
Phase 6 checklist confirmed by user with summary: pass.

Phase 6 implementation completed: added provider-neutral office execution contract helpers, normalized Codex and Claude Code server chat results through that contract, and added reference-style Codex provider facade methods over the existing app-server bridge bottom layer.

Phase 6 tests passed: py_compile, provider execution contract, Codex provider/server/run SSE, Claude Code server/run SSE, project execution, meeting blocker, provider runtime config, and source-level UI checks.

Phase 7 requested by user: extract codex_bridge generic JSONL app-server runtime into a reusable layer, then implement Codex-specific app-server protocol layer using the reference branch as primary guide while preserving provider facade and office contract.
Phase 7 review note, checklist CHK-161 through CHK-174, and todolist TODO-102 through TODO-111 added; waiting for user confirmation before implementation.
Phase 7 checklist confirmed by user with summary: go.
Phase 7 implementation completed: added provider-neutral JsonlAppServerRuntime and refactored codex_bridge.py to use it for transport/RPC mechanics while preserving Codex protocol behavior.

Phase 7 tests passed: py_compile, fake app-server runtime tests, Codex provider/server/run SSE, provider execution contract, Claude Code run SSE, project execution, meeting blocker, provider runtime config, and source-level UI checks.
Phase 8 requested and confirmed by user: split Codex protocol adapter into app/providers/codex_app_server.py, keep codex_bridge.py as compatibility shim, and run tests/regressions.
Phase 8 implementation completed: app/providers/codex_app_server.py owns Codex protocol behavior; app/providers/codex_bridge.py is a compatibility shim re-exporting the existing public API.

Phase 8 tests passed: py_compile, Codex adapter split source check, fake runtime tests, Codex provider/server/run SSE, provider execution contract, Claude Code run SSE, project execution, meeting blocker, provider runtime config, and source-level UI checks.
Phase 9 requested by user: document Codex app-server run-state parity work to align with reference CodexAppRunState, tokenUsage, pending approval store, and first-class native send_chat_message path.
Phase 9 review note, checklist CHK-182 through CHK-191, and todolist TODO-117 through TODO-124 added; waiting for user confirmation before implementation.
Phase 9 checklist confirmed by user with summary: ok.
Phase 9 implementation completed: CodexAppRunState added to app/providers/codex_app_server.py with reply/tools/thinking/approval/tokenUsage snapshots and final result augmentation.

Phase 9 pending approval store completed: adapter pending_approval/respond_approval now resolves the same active interaction requests used by legacy respond(), and fixture reply_text compatibility remains unchanged.

Phase 9 tests passed: py_compile, Codex fake app-server bridge, Codex provider/server/run-SSE, provider execution contract, generic app-server runtime, Claude Code run-SSE/provider, Hermes API client, project execution, meeting blocker, provider runtime config, and source-level UI checks.

Phase 9 residual reference difference: local implementation intentionally keeps the shared JsonlAppServerRuntime and existing ProviderRunBridge/SSE compatibility instead of wholesale adopting the reference monolithic Codex provider loop.

Phase 10 requested by user: plan continued bottom-layer alignment against eliautobot/main for Codex and Claude Code while preserving local ProviderRunBridge, provider_execution contract, project/meeting/archive/scheduled behavior, and recent roster/name fixes.
Phase 10 review note, checklist CHK-192 through CHK-206, and todolist TODO-125 through TODO-135 added; waiting for user confirmation before implementation.
Phase 10 checklist confirmed by user with summary: pass.

Phase 10 implementation completed: Codex app-server auth/test now probes initialize plus account/read and returns reference-compatible auth metadata; Claude Code test/auth now prefers claude auth status --json with version fallback; local ProviderRunBridge/SSE, provider_execution contract, project/meeting/archive/scheduled boundaries remain authoritative.

Phase 10 tests passed: py_compile, Codex/Claude provider and server tests, Codex bridge, Codex/Claude run SSE, provider execution contract, provider runtime config, project execution, meeting blocker and phase regressions, scheduled cron regressions, and provider/chat/source UI checks.

Phase 10 residual reference difference: local implementation intentionally keeps shared JsonlAppServerRuntime, ProviderRunBridge/SSE distribution, and office-facing normalization instead of adopting the reference branch's monolithic provider loop.

Phase 10 Chrome MCP real E2E completed against local 8090 with real Codex and Claude Code CLIs. MCP found and verified a Codex run history persistence race fix: Codex /runs now sets human source defaults and persists assistant replies immediately from terminal activity, so closing/reopening chat can reload both user message and Codex reply.

Phase 11 review and checklist drafted after fresh eliautobot/main comparison. Remaining reference merge targets are Codex approval APIs, provider progress history, Hermes native runs/SSE, native model/auth backend helpers, and small Codex protocol parity gaps while preserving local layered architecture and i18n.
Phase 11 checklist confirmed by user with summary: ok. Phase 11 todolist TODO-136 through TODO-149 created and implementation started per user request: do it,注意做好测试跟验收.

Phase 11 partial implementation verified: Codex approval server APIs and Hermes native /runs/events/stop now use existing local provider facades and ProviderRunBridge. TODO-136, TODO-137, TODO-142, TODO-145, TODO-146, TODO-147, TODO-148, and TODO-149 completed; deeper UI/progress-history/native model-auth parity TODOs remain deferred.

Phase 12 review, checklist CHK-225 through CHK-241, and todolist TODO-150 through TODO-159 added for provider progress history parity. Checklist confirmed by user with summary: pass. Implementation not started.

Phase 12 implementation completed: generic provider progress history helper, Codex/Claude Code/Hermes progress persistence, frontend active progress restore, and regression verification passed. Codex approval UI wiring to new pending/respond APIs remains deferred as TODO-156/CHK-235.

Phase 12 follow-up Chrome MCP E2E completed after user asked whether end-to-end acceptance was actually done. The recheck found and fixed stale terminal Codex progress events in communication history; final MCP evidence shows real Codex /runs + SSE + browser reload returns exactly the user request and one Codex OK reply with threadId, turnId, modifiedFiles=[], and no codex-progress residue.

Phase 13 review and checklist CHK-242 through CHK-260 added after refetching eliautobot/main at eb119493/v0.6.30. Scope: selective bottom-layer parity follow-up for Codex approval UI, Codex app-server protocol details, Claude Code native provider parity, and native provider settings, while preserving local project/meeting/archive/i18n boundaries. Waiting for user checklist confirmation before todolist creation.
Phase 13 checklist confirmed by user with summary: pass. Creating Phase 13 todolist from CHK-242 through CHK-260.
Phase 13 todolist TODO-160 through TODO-172 created from confirmed checklist.
Phase 13 implementation completed: Codex approval UI now uses approval/pending and approval/respond while preserving legacy interaction fallback.
Phase 13 Codex protocol parity completed: command/file/permission approval response mapping is covered by tests and shared by legacy and reference-style approval paths.
Phase 13 Claude Code parity completed: non-main profiles use profile workspace/custom registry plus --agent, and stream-json JSON argument deltas are parsed.

Phase 13 tests passed across provider runtime, project/meeting/archive source checks, git diff --check, and isolated HTTP/SSE/history E2E. Chrome MCP browser was unavailable due locked shared profile, so the temporary service was stopped after fallback E2E.
Phase 14 final closure review and checklist CHK-261 through CHK-274 drafted after user clarified this should be the last phase and should merge all remaining safe reference bottom-layer behavior.
Phase 14 final closure checklist confirmed by user with summary: pass. TODO-173 through TODO-183 created from CHK-261 through CHK-274.

Phase 14 implementation completed: Codex approval respond now persists duplicate-protected result history and emits approval.responded presence events; Claude Code provider exposes reference-compatible on_progress snapshots; local layered provider architecture and office workflows remain preserved.

Phase 14 tests passed: provider/runtime Python matrix, office workflow regressions, source-level UI checks, git diff --check, and Chrome MCP E2E against temporary service on 8152 for Codex and Claude Code run SSE/history. Temporary service was stopped.

Phase 15 requested by user: merge reference Codex native app-server core (CodexAppServerClient, JSON-RPC stdio lifecycle, approval handling, CodexAppRunState, tool/reasoning/token parsing) into the current layered implementation instead of whole-file replacement. Phase 15 requirement addendum, review, and checklist CHK-275 through CHK-289 drafted; waiting for user confirmation before todolist creation.
Phase 15 checklist confirmed by user with summary: pass. Phase 15 todolist TODO-184 through TODO-193 created from CHK-275 through CHK-289; ready for implementation planning.

Phase 15 implementation completed: reference Codex native app-server core behavior merged into local layered runtime/client, including stderr lifecycle diagnostics, initialize capabilities, approval request/response parity, profile-aware pending approvals, modified file shape compatibility, and run-state/result preservation.

Phase 15 tests passed: provider app-server runtime, Codex bridge/provider/server/run-SSE, provider execution contract, project execution, meeting blocker, Codex run/approval/source checks, node syntax, and git diff --check. Real Codex/Chrome MCP E2E was not run in this slice; fixture-backed app-server tests covered the merged core behavior.
Phase 15 MCP E2E follow-up initially found a real Codex app-server recovery issue: a subsequent run could fail with App-server request timed out: thread/start after a prior successful run.
Phase 15 recovery fix completed: CodexAppServerClient serializes run/compact operations per client and restarts/retries app-server once when thread/start or thread/resume times out.

Phase 15 real Chrome MCP E2E passed after the fix: two consecutive real Codex /api/codex/runs requests emitted run.started -> provider.activity -> run.completed and history persisted both human request and Codex reply with real threadId/turnId.

Phase 16 requested by user as the final reference feature closure phase: merge all remaining safe details from eliautobot/main, preserve local layered architecture and VO project/meeting/archive/scheduled behavior, and produce a final diff closure report. Phase 16 requirement addendum, review, and checklist CHK-290 through CHK-315 drafted; waiting for user confirmation before todolist creation.
Phase 16 checklist confirmed by user with summary: pass. Phase 16 todolist TODO-194 through TODO-214 created from CHK-290 through CHK-315.

Phase 16 implementation completed: Claude Code native user-agent discovery now uses profile workspace defaults, VO Claude office-agent discovery now requires office-agent.json, and Hermes native run/SSE visible-thinking handling no longer references a result before it exists.

Phase 16 final verification passed: provider/runtime tests, VO project/meeting regressions, Node/source UI checks, git diff --check, and HTTP/SSE E2E against temporary latest-code service on 8156. Temporary service was stopped.

Phase 16 Chrome MCP real browser E2E follow-up passed on temporary latest-code service 8158: browser-context provider metadata, Claude Code run/history, and real Codex run/SSE/history all passed; temporary service was stopped.

Phase 16 final closure report completed: remaining differences against cached remotes/eliautobot/main at eb119493 are intentional local architecture/product-boundary differences rather than pending safe provider-runtime feature gaps.

latest_verification.scope: Chrome MCP real browser end-to-end validation plus final selective merge closure against cached remotes/eliautobot/main at eb119493.
latest_verification.implemented: Claude Code native user-agent profile workspace parity; Claude Code VO office-agent directory filtering by office-agent.json; Hermes native chat/SSE visible-thinking result handling fix; final closure report and acceptance evidence.
latest_verification.mcp_checks: Temporary service http://127.0.0.1:8158 loaded through Chrome MCP. Browser-context /config/providers returned native provider metadata for Hermes, Codex, and Claude Code without secrets. Browser-context Claude Code /api/claude-code/runs completed and history for phase16-mcp-claude persisted exactly phase16 mcp claude hello plus Claude MCP OK phase16 with no progress residue. Browser-context Codex /api/codex/runs started runId=codex-1782820036161-cd8ad5f7; SSE returned run.started, provider.activity, keepalives, run.completed; final reply OK_phase16_mcp_codex_2; history for phase16-mcp-codex-2 persisted exactly request plus reply with threadId=019f185a-e306-7e72-9466-1477e056af99, turnId=019f185b-2230-77b3-b624-38ae2cfda6d2, modifiedFiles=[], and no progress residue. Temporary service stopped.
latest_verification.known_nonblocking: Project execution regression logged expected gateway WebSocket failures and one background TemporaryDirectory cleanup FileNotFoundError after assertions passed. Meeting phase tests logged expected gateway failures. Chrome MCP page showed expected /pc-metrics 502 noise because the metrics backend is not available in this fixture.
